result.summary: Iterable exposes a REST API, a local npm-packaged MCP server, and an npm-packaged CLI; all use Iterable API keys, with the REST API also supporting JWT-enabled API keys plus per-user Bearer JWTs.
result.description: Iterable is a customer engagement and cross-channel messaging platform used to manage users, campaigns, templates, journeys, catalogs, and related marketing data. It provides tooling for sending and tracking email, push, SMS, in-app, and embedded messaging interactions.

iterable_api_key.label: Iterable API key
iterable_api_key.setup: In an Iterable project, create an API key as described in [API Keys](https://support.iterable.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043464871-API-Keys). Choose a key type and permissions appropriate for your use case and data center. Keys are project-specific and USDC keys work with `https://api.iterable.com`, while EDC keys work with `https://api.eu.iterable.com`.

iterable_jwt_enabled_key.label: JWT-enabled Iterable API key plus per-user JWT
iterable_jwt_enabled_key.setup: Create an API key with JWT authentication enabled per [API Keys](https://support.iterable.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043464871-API-Keys), then on your server generate a per-user JWT as described in [JWT-Enabled API Keys](https://support.iterable.com/hc/en-us/articles/360050801231-JWT-Enabled-API-Keys). The JWT must be signed with the API key's shared secret and sent as a `Bearer` token for requests that use the JWT-enabled key.

variables.name: data_center
variables.resolveFrom: Use `https://api.iterable.com` for USDC projects or `https://api.eu.iterable.com` for EDC projects, matching where the API key was created.
variables.description: Iterable project data center determines the API hostname.
